headline: Big consulting firms are racing AI adoption but hitting a wall: cheaper AI agents replacing $500K reports, consultants burnt out on shortened timelines

stories.title: PE Firms Replace $500K McKinsey Due Diligence with $50K AI Agents in Live Deals
stories.summary: DiligenceSquared, a five-month-old startup founded by an ex-BCG consultant, just got $2+ trillion in combined AUM from PE firms to replace traditional consulting with AI-powered interviews—literally cutting costs by 90% on production deals with real money at risk.
stories.takeaway: AI is lowkey dismantling the consulting pyramid: McKinsey's whole scarcity-based value prop just got undercut, and now PE firms are field-testing AI on billion-dollar deals before anyone even knows if it actually works.

stories.title: OpenAI Announces 'Frontier Alliances' with McKinsey, BCG, and Accenture to Drive Enterprise AI Adoption
stories.summary: OpenAI dropped multi-year partnerships with McKinsey, BCG, Accenture, and Capgemini to help enterprises actually deploy AI agents at scale—basically weaponizing consultants to sell OpenAI's tech stack to their clients.
stories.takeaway: Big consulting firms are becoming OpenAI's distribution network fr fr. They'll co-develop solutions, but the real play is consultants convincing clients to change workflows around OpenAI products.

stories.title: Accenture's 8% Stock Jump: Why Sovereign AI & Mistral Partnership Signals Physical AI Era
stories.summary: Accenture surged after announcing a multi-year deal with Mistral AI plus acquiring Verum Partners—market's betting hard that consulting's future is "Physical AI" (robotics, infrastructure) not chatbots, and Accenture's playing both sides.
stories.takeaway: Accenture tbh just positioned itself as the infrastructure play in AI consulting while others are stuck in chatbot land. Wall Street thinks consultants can't be replaced if they're building actual factories and power grids.

watchNext.title: How AI is Creating a Rift at McKinsey, BCG, and Bain: Faster Timelines, Vanishing Creativity
watchNext.why: Junior consultants lowkey hate their AI 'productivity tools' because partners are now demanding work in hours instead of weeks—literally creating burnout while claiming to save time.

watchNext.title: Deloitte's 'Modernization': Scrapping Job Titles, Shifting to Agentic Workforce by June 2026
watchNext.why: Deloitte's literally restructuring how it defines consultants (alphanumeric levels instead of titles) because AI agents are replacing the pyramid model—McKinsey's fleet of AI agents grew 500% in 18 months.
